response:
Find the minimum value of the function y = e^x - x  
THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION y = e^x - x IS FOUND BY TAKING THE DERIVATIVE OF THE FUNCTION AND SETTING IT EQUAL TO ZERO THE DERIVATIVE OF y = e^x - x IS dy/dx = e^x - 1 SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES e^x - 1 = 0 SOLVING FOR x GIVES e^x = 1 AND SO x = 0 TO CONFIRM THIS IS A MINIMUM TAKE THE SECOND DERIVATIVE WHICH IS d^2y/dx^2 = e^x SINCE e^x IS ALWAYS POSITIVE THE FUNCTION IS CONCAVE UP AT x = 0 HENCE x = 0 IS A MINIMUM PLUGGING x = 0 INTO THE ORIGINAL FUNCTION GIVES y = e^0 - 0 = 1 - 0 = 1 THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION IS 1  
FAILURE
